name: axiom-static-analysis-engineering
description: Building static analyzers as engines, not running them as users. AST → IR → analysis → reporting pipelines: visitation patterns, abstract-domain (taint lattice) design, three-phase inference (variable → function → callgraph), plugin architecture for rules, false-positive economics with auditable suppression, and the static-vs-runtime boundary. v0.1.0 ships router + 6 sheets covering the architectural spine and operational reality; manifest-driven configuration, SARIF/CI integration, callgraph-resolution depth, cross-module flow, decorator-as-assertion, scaling/incremental analysis, LLM-assisted rule explanation, plus 3 commands and 2 agents are scheduled for v0.2.0. Pattern pack — applies wherever you build an analyzer (linting, taint, contracts, governance, entity extraction).
